basics.name: Zerui "Jerry" Ma
basics.label: Prospect Ph.D. Student
basics.summary: Currently finishing a Bachelor in CS, Math and Data Science. Interested in academic research. Click the PDF icon to download my resume.

publications.title: Journal of Psychopathology and Clinical Science
publications.journal: Journal of Psychopathology and Clinical Science

publications.title: Transactions of the Association for Computational Linguistics (TACL)

research experience.name: Personality LLM Research
research experience.summary: Using PyTorch and Transformer to fine-tune a custom LLM to predict Big-Five Personality Traits. Involved in preprocessing real-life interview data and fine-tuning BERT and Llama models.
highlights: Extracted fine-tuned roBERTa embeddings for Recurrent Neural Networks training.
Ran batch jobs on HPC and managed complex data structures using Ubuntu Linux commands.

research experience.name: Recommender Systems Research
research experience.summary: Conducting research for the Robert Mayer Undergraduate Research Fellowship Program, developing a recommendation system using Set-Cover Algorithms to improve university curriculum advising.
highlights: Built a relational database with web-scraped data.
Developed a system for retrieving accurate course recommendations based on student history.

fellowship grants.title: Robert Mayer Undergraduate Research Fellowship
fellowship grants.summary: Robert Mayer, Jr. founded the Mayer Undergraduate Research Fellows Program in 2012 with the goal of supporting Dedman College undergraduate students in pursuing ambitious interdisciplinary research. Mr. Mayer’s nephew, Bruce Sternberg, continues to support the program today.

teaching experience.name: Governor's Champion Summer Camp
teaching experience.summary: Developed and taught an AI and Machine Learning course for high school students, covering foundational concepts such as intelligent agents, the history of AI, data preprocessing, and machine learning algorithms.
highlights: Implemented hands-on projects to help students apply theoretical knowledge to practical applications.
Gained valuable experience in curriculum design, public speaking, and academic coordination.

projects.name: Personality LLM Research
projects.summary: Using PyTorch and Transformer to fine-tune a custom LLM to predict Big-Five Personality Traits. Involved in preprocessing real-life interview data and fine-tuning BERT and Llama models.
highlights: Extracted fine-tuned roBERTa embeddings for Recurrent Neural Networks training.
Ran batch jobs on HPC and managed complex data structures using Ubuntu Linux commands.

projects.name: Individual Research - Recommender Systems
projects.summary: Conducting research as part of the Robert Mayer Undergraduate Research Fellowship Program, building a recommendation system using Set-Cover Algorithms to improve university curriculum advising.
highlights: Built a relational database with web-scraped data.
Developed a system for retrieving accurate course recommendations based on student history.

projects.name: Governor's Champion Summer Camp
projects.summary: Taught a course for high school students on AI and machine learning, covering topics such as intelligent agents, data preprocessing, and algorithms.
highlights: Developed the curriculum and coordinated with colleagues to deliver engaging hands-on projects.

projects.name: RAG Application Full Stack Development
projects.summary: Developed a medical transcript summarization tool using LangChain and Python. Hosted a vector database to enhance LLM-based medical diagnosis using GPT-4.
highlights: Increased medical diagnosis efficiency by streamlining review of patient histories.

work experience.name: Dedman Center for Lifetime Sports
work experience.summary: Managed a team to maintain the facility and ensured smooth operations with strong communication and leadership skills.

additional information.summary: Open to opportunities in computational linguistics, natural language processing, and ML research. Interested in contributing to research groups focused on large language models, their optimization, and their application.
